title: Saudi-Arabian Intensely Peppery Dill Pickles Gratin
description: A crunchy, flavorful gratin dish that combines the tanginess of dill pickles with the pungency of black pepper.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced dill pickles to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes, or until they start to brown.
Sprinkle the flour over the pickles and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually pour in the milk, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it thickens.
Stir in the shredded cheddar cheese until melted and smooth.
Transfer the pickle mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the black pepper and salt over the top of the pickles.
Dot the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over the top of the pickles.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill and serve hot.
